name: Checkbox
purpose: Allows the user to select one or more items from a set.
whenToUse: To select multiple options from a list.
To agree to terms and conditions.
To toggle a boolean value within a form where explicit submission is expected.
whenNotToUse: When only one option can be selected from a mutually exclusive list (use Radio).
When changes should take effect immediately without requiring a save or submit action (use Switch).

accessibility.touchTargets: Ensure minimum touch target of 48x48dp, usually achieved by wrapping the Checkbox in a CheckboxListTile or adding padding.
accessibility.semantics: Always associate the checkbox with a descriptive label using CheckboxListTile or Semantics so screen readers can announce its purpose.
accessibility.stateFeedback: Screen readers automatically announce the checked, unchecked, or mixed state based on the value property.

flutterMappingNotes: Map the checked or unchecked state to the `value` property (true = checked, false = unchecked).
Use the `onChanged` callback to handle state updates and trigger rebuilds.
The checkbox automatically enters the 'disabled' state when `onChanged` is set to null.
Set `tristate: true` to allow `value` to be null, representing an indeterminate/mixed state (useful for 'select all' parent checkboxes).
Prefer using `CheckboxListTile` to easily combine a Checkbox with a Text label and expand the touch target to the full row.
Map box.checked.background to `activeColor` or use `fillColor` (MaterialStateProperty) for more granular state control.
Map box.error.border to `side` (BorderSide) using MaterialState.error, or use the `isError` property on CheckboxListTile if applicable.
Use `overlayColor` (MaterialStateProperty) to define visual feedback for pressed, hovered, and focus states.
